story: On a rushing morning in New York, Zena heard her name and turned: Elijah, the man she had loved and lost five years ago. He said he was in town for a week, leaving tomorrow, and lifted a pink doll: “For my daughter, Naomi.” They traded small talk. “Hope we meet again,” he said. As he walked up, Elijah had slid a business card into her jacket, the back scrawled: “If you ever want to talk—6 p.m., Café Orpheus, today.” Zena, stung, excused herself for a meeting and walked away, tears threatening but her steps steady.
implausible_knowledge: As he walked up, Elijah had slid a business card into her jacket, the back scrawled: “If you ever want to talk—6 p.m., Café Orpheus, today.”
question: What will she most plausibly do?
answer_with_knowledge: Plan to go to Café Orpheus at 6 p.m. to talk with Elijah.
answer_without_knowledge: Put the phone away, head to her meeting, and avoid contacting Elijah.
